speaker: RODERIGO
text: Iago—

speaker: IAGO
text: What sayst thou, noble heart?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: What will I do, think’st thou?

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, go to bed and sleep.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I will incontinently drown myself.

speaker: IAGO
text: If thou dost, I shall never love thee after. Why,

speaker: IAGO
text: thou silly gentleman!

speaker: RODERIGO
text: It is silliness to live, when to live is torment,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: and then have we a prescription to die when death is

speaker: RODERIGO
text: our physician.

speaker: IAGO
text: O, villainous! I have looked upon the world for

speaker: IAGO
text: four times seven years, and since I could distinguish

speaker: IAGO
text: betwixt a benefit and an injury, I never found

speaker: IAGO
text: man that knew how to love himself. Ere I would say

speaker: IAGO
text: I would drown myself for the love of a guinea hen, I

speaker: IAGO
text: would change my humanity with a baboon.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: What should I do? I confess it is my shame

speaker: RODERIGO
text: to be so fond, but it is not in my virtue to amend it.

speaker: IAGO
text: Virtue? A fig! ’Tis in ourselves that we are thus or

speaker: IAGO
text: thus. Our bodies are our gardens, to the which our

speaker: IAGO
text: wills are gardeners. So that if we will plant nettles

speaker: IAGO
text: or sow lettuce, set hyssop and weed up thyme,

speaker: IAGO
text: supply it with one gender of herbs or distract it

speaker: IAGO
text: with many, either to have it sterile with idleness or

speaker: IAGO
text: manured with industry, why the power and corrigible

speaker: IAGO
text: authority of this lies in our wills. If the balance

speaker: IAGO
text: of our lives had not one scale of reason to poise

speaker: IAGO
text: another of sensuality, the blood and baseness of our

speaker: IAGO
text: natures would conduct us to most prepost’rous

speaker: IAGO
text: conclusions. But we have reason to cool our raging

speaker: IAGO
text: motions, our carnal stings, our unbitted lusts—

speaker: IAGO
text: whereof I take this that you call love to be a sect, or

speaker: IAGO
text: scion.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: It cannot be.

speaker: IAGO
text: It is merely a lust of the blood and a permission

speaker: IAGO
text: of the will. Come, be a man! Drown thyself? Drown

speaker: IAGO
text: cats and blind puppies. I have professed me thy

speaker: IAGO
text: friend, and I confess me knit to thy deserving

speaker: IAGO
text: with cables of perdurable toughness. I could never

speaker: IAGO
text: better stead thee than now. Put money in thy purse.

speaker: IAGO
text: Follow thou the wars; defeat thy favor with an

speaker: IAGO
text: usurped beard. I say, put money in thy purse. It

speaker: IAGO
text: cannot be that Desdemona should long continue

speaker: IAGO
text: her love to the Moor—put money in thy purse—

speaker: IAGO
text: nor he his to her. It was a violent commencement in

speaker: IAGO
text: her, and thou shalt see an answerable sequestration

speaker: IAGO
text: —put but money in thy purse. These Moors are

speaker: IAGO
text: changeable in their wills. Fill thy purse with money.

speaker: IAGO
text: The food that to him now is as luscious as locusts

speaker: IAGO
text: shall be to him shortly as bitter as coloquintida.

speaker: IAGO
text: She must change for youth. When she is sated

speaker: IAGO
text: with his body she will find the error of her choice.

speaker: IAGO
text: Therefore, put money in thy purse. If thou wilt

speaker: IAGO
text: needs damn thyself, do it a more delicate way than

speaker: IAGO
text: drowning. Make all the money thou canst. If sanctimony

speaker: IAGO
text: and a frail vow betwixt an erring barbarian

speaker: IAGO
text: and a supersubtle Venetian be not too hard for my

speaker: IAGO
text: wits and all the tribe of hell, thou shalt enjoy her.

speaker: IAGO
text: Therefore make money. A pox of drowning thyself!

speaker: IAGO
text: It is clean out of the way. Seek thou rather to be

speaker: IAGO
text: hanged in compassing thy joy than to be drowned

speaker: IAGO
text: and go without her.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Wilt thou be fast to my hopes if I depend on

speaker: RODERIGO
text: the issue?

speaker: IAGO
text: Thou art sure of me. Go, make money. I have

speaker: IAGO
text: told thee often, and I retell thee again and again, I

speaker: IAGO
text: hate the Moor. My cause is hearted; thine hath no

speaker: IAGO
text: less reason. Let us be conjunctive in our revenge

speaker: IAGO
text: against him. If thou canst cuckold him, thou dost

speaker: IAGO
text: thyself a pleasure, me a sport. There are many

speaker: IAGO
text: events in the womb of time which will be delivered.

speaker: IAGO
text: Traverse, go, provide thy money. We will have more

speaker: IAGO
text: of this tomorrow. Adieu.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Where shall we meet i’ th’ morning?

speaker: IAGO
text: At my lodging.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I’ll be with thee betimes.

speaker: IAGO
text: Go to, farewell. Do you hear, Roderigo?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: What say you?

speaker: IAGO
text: No more of drowning, do you hear?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I am changed.

speaker: IAGO
text: Go to, farewell. Put money enough in your

speaker: IAGO
text: purse.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I’ll sell all my land.

speaker: IAGO
text: Thus do I ever make my fool my purse.

speaker: IAGO
text: For I mine own gained knowledge should profane

speaker: IAGO
text: If I would time expend with such a snipe

speaker: IAGO
text: But for my sport and profit. I hate the Moor,

speaker: IAGO
text: And it is thought abroad that ’twixt my sheets

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Has done my office. I know not if ’t be true,

speaker: IAGO
text: But I, for mere suspicion in that kind,

speaker: IAGO
text: Will do as if for surety. He holds me well.

speaker: IAGO
text: The better shall my purpose work on him.

speaker: IAGO
text: Cassio’s a proper man. Let me see now:

speaker: IAGO
text: To get his place and to plume up my will

speaker: IAGO
text: In double knavery—How? how?—Let’s see.

speaker: IAGO
text: After some time, to abuse Othello’s ear

speaker: IAGO
text: That he is too familiar with his wife.

speaker: IAGO
text: He hath a person and a smooth dispose

speaker: IAGO
text: To be suspected, framed to make women false.

speaker: IAGO
text: The Moor is of a free and open nature

speaker: IAGO
text: That thinks men honest that but seem to be so,

speaker: IAGO
text: And will as tenderly be led by th’ nose

speaker: IAGO
text: As asses are.

speaker: IAGO
text: I have ’t. It is engendered. Hell and night

speaker: IAGO
text: Must bring this monstrous birth to the world’s light.
